title: Feature Metadata
description: JSON Schema for features' `metadata.yaml` files. This is a superset of the devcontainer-feature.json schema at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devcontainers/spec/main/schemas/devContainerFeature.schema.json`, extended with internal-use-only properties (prefixed with an underscore) for richer metadata and documentation generation. All 'description' fields support simple (links, formatting) Markdown and HTML.

id.description: ID of the Feature. The id should be unique in the context of the repository/published package where the feature exists and must match the name of the directory where the devcontainer-feature.json resides.

version.description: The version of the Feature. Follows the semanatic versioning (semver) specification.

keywords.description: List of strings relevant to a user that would search for this Feature.

options.description: Possible user-configurable options for this Feature. The selected options will be passed as environment variables when installing the Feature into the container.

_unsupported_platforms.description: List of platforms where this Feature cannot be installed.

arch.description: CPU architecture from uname -m: x86_64, aarch64, armv7l, i686, etc.

id.description: OS ID from /etc/os-release (or 'macos' on macOS): ubuntu, debian, alpine, fedora, arch, rhel, opensuse-leap, macos, etc.

id_like.description: OS ID_LIKE from /etc/os-release (or 'macos' on macOS): debian, rhel, arch, suse, etc.

reason.description: Reason why the platform is unsupported, for display in supporting tools.

_dependencies.description: Build and runtime dependency manifests for the Feature.

build.description: Build dependencies. These are only required when installing the Feature, and can be removed afterwards to save space.

dependsOn.description: An object of Feature dependencies that must be satisified before this Feature is installed. Elements follow the same semantics of the features object in devcontainer.json

installsAfter.description: Array of ID's of Features that should execute before this one. Allows control for feature authors on soft dependencies between different Features.

legacyIds.description: Array of old IDs used to publish this Feature. The property is useful for renaming a currently published Feature within a single namespace.

deprecated.description: Indicates that the Feature is deprecated, and will not receive any further updates/support. This property is intended to be used by the supporting tools for highlighting Feature deprecation.

capAdd.description: Passes docker capabilities to include when creating the dev container.

customizations.description: Tool-specific configuration. Each tool should use a JSON object subproperty with a unique name to group its customizations.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint script that should fire at container start up.

init.description: Adds the tiny init process to the container (--init) when the Feature is used.

mounts.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

privileged.description: Sets privileged mode (--privileged) for the container.

securityOpt.description: Sets container security options to include when creating the container.

onCreateCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container. This command is run after "initializeCommand" and before "updateContentCommand". If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. If this is an object, each provided command will be run in parallel.

updateContentCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container and rerun when the workspace content was updated while creating the container. This command is run after "onCreateCommand" and before "postCreateCommand". If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. If this is an object, each provided command will be run in parallel.

postCreateCommand.description: A command to run after creating the container. This command is run after "updateContentCommand" and before "postStartCommand". If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. If this is an object, each provided command will be run in parallel.

postStartCommand.description: A command to run after starting the container. This command is run after "postCreateCommand" and before "postAttachCommand". If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. If this is an object, each provided command will be run in parallel.

postAttachCommand.description: A command to run when attaching to the container. This command is run after "postStartCommand". If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell. If this is an object, each provided command will be run in parallel.

FeatureOption.description: Feature option. Two internal-use-only array types are added in addition to the standard boolean and string types for script generation purposes. Externally, these array types are represented as string options where array values are newline-delimited.

anyOf.description: Option value is represented with a boolean value.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

type.description: The type of the option. Can be 'boolean' or 'string'.  Options of type 'string' should use the 'enum' or 'proposals' property to provide a list of allowed values.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

enum.description: Allowed values for this option.  Unlike 'proposals', the user cannot provide a custom value not included in the 'enum' array.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

type.description: The type of the option. Can be 'boolean' or 'string'.  Options of type 'string' should use the 'enum' or 'proposals' property to provide a list of allowed values.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

proposals.description: Suggested values for this option.  Unlike 'enum', the 'proposals' attribute indicates the installation script can handle arbitrary values provided by the user.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

type.description: The type of the option. Can be 'boolean' or 'string'.  Options of type 'string' should use the 'enum' or 'proposals' property to provide a list of allowed values.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

enum.description: Allowed values for this option.  Unlike 'proposals', the user cannot provide a custom value not included in the 'enum' array.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

type.description: The type of the option. Can be 'boolean' or 'string'.  Options of type 'string' should use the 'enum' or 'proposals' property to provide a list of allowed values.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

proposals.description: Suggested values for this option.  Unlike 'enum', the 'proposals' attribute indicates the installation script can handle arbitrary values provided by the user.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

type.description: The type of the option. Can be 'boolean' or 'string'.  Options of type 'string' should use the 'enum' or 'proposals' property to provide a list of allowed values.

Mount.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

type.description: Type of mount. Can be 'bind' or 'volume'.
